perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may, with written notice, be terminated by either party should Turpin's employment with the Company terminate for any reason. Termination under this Section 12 shall be effective from the date written notice is delivered or the date specified in the written notice. whichever is later.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may, with written notice, be terminated by either party should Turpin's employment with the Company terminate for any reason. Termination under this Section 12 shall be effective immediately upon delivery of written notice, regardless of the date specified in the notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows termination to be effective on the date of notice or a later specified date. The changed text contradicts this by stating that termination is effective immediately upon delivery of notice, nullifying the option of a later effective date. This creates an in-text contradiction that causes uncertainty about when termination takes effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue until terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may, with written notice, be terminated by either party should Turpin's employment with the Company terminate for any reason. Termination under this Section 12 shall be effective immediately upon delivery of written notice, regardless of the date specified in the notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies that termination is effective immediately upon delivery of written notice, regardless of the date specified in the notice. This directly contradicts the original text, which allowed for termination to be effective either on the date of notice or a later date specified in the notice. Since the Contradicted Text matches the Changed Text, it is clear that the change introduces a direct conflict within the agreement about when termination is effective, leading to uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin does hereby assign and transfer to the Company, effective upon creation, all right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and does hereby assign all of Turpin's future right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to each of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, effective at the time each is created.
perturbation.changed_text: Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin does hereby grant a non-exclusive license to the Company, effective upon creation, for the use of all Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, but retains ownership.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Turpin assigns and transfers all right, title, and interest in the Work Product to the Company, making the Company the exclusive owner. The changed text contradicts this by stating that Turpin grants a non-exclusive license to the Company but retains ownership of the Work Product. This creates an in-text contradiction about who owns the Work Product and the rights associated with it, making the ownership status unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin does hereby grant a non-exclusive license to the Company, effective upon creation, for the use of all Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, but retains ownership.
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the original intent and the contradicted text by stating that Turpin grants a non-exclusive license to the Company for the Work Product but retains ownership, whereas previously, Turpin assigned all rights and ownership to the company, making the company the exclusive owner. This is a direct in-text contradiction concerning ownership of the work product, which falls under the definition of inconsistencies creating an in-text contradiction.
